OSINTdefender Speaking earlier tonight on Fox News, U.S. Vice President JD Vance presented a more optimistic and positive view of this past weekend’s peace talks with Iran in Islamabad then he gave on Sunday, stating that while “things went wrong,” they also “went right” during the negotiations and that progress was made with Iran. Vance added that, “We made a lot of progress, but we also made very clear, and I think this is part of the progress we made, what the terms where the United States could make some accommodation, what terms we were flexible on, and what things we absolutely needed to see in order for the President of the United States to feel like he was getting a good deal.” tweet

⚡️ 🇮🇩 🇷🇺 Indonesia’s President Prabowo Subianto met with Russian President Vladimir Putin during a state visit to Moscow, where the two discussed global geopolitical developments and ways to strengthen cooperation in energy and the economy. Subianto says he thanked Russia for its support of Indonesia’s entry into BRICS: During my state visit to Russia, I met with the President of Russia, Vladimir Putin, to discuss the latest global geopolitical dynamics and to strengthen mutually beneficial strategic cooperation in the fields of energy and the economy. In that meeting, I also expressed my appreciation and gratitude for Russia's support of Indonesia's membership in BRICS. This membership opens new opportunities for strengthening the partnership between our two countries, particularly in expanding cooperation in priority sectors, as well as reinforcing Indonesia's role in a more inclusive and balanced global economic architecture. This meeting also forms part of my active diplomacy efforts to advance national interests and enhance the welfare of the Indonesian people. @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to OSINT OSINTdefender RT @ianellisjones : POTUS declared over the weekend that the U.S. Navy “will begin the process of BLOCKADING any and all Ships trying to enter, or leave, the Strait of Hormuz,” which followed Central Command's announcement that two destroyers executed a mine clearance mission and asserted navigational rights and freedoms in the Strait. “Additional U.S. forces, including underwater drones, will join the clearance effort in the coming days,” the release concluded. It isn’t clear exactly what the destroyers did near the Strait or the details of their attempted transit. At 10:00 ET on 13 April, USCENTCOM began implementing the “blockade of all maritime traffic entering and exiting Iranian ports.” Additional warships have arrived in Europe and the Middle East over the last week, and more are en route, as the U.S. takes advantage of the ceasefire to rearm, resupply, and reposition key assets across theaters. The first elements of the George H.W. Bush CSG transited the Strait of Gibraltar in early April, and the flagship with at least three escorts is expected to follow in the near term. The Gerald R. Ford CSG is on station in the eastern Mediterranean Sea, most recently conducting operations southwest of Cyprus, satellite imagery shows. The Boxer ARG, embarked with the 11th Marine Expeditionary Unit (MEU), departed Pearl Harbor and is transiting the Pacific Ocean westbound toward the Middle East, where the Tripoli ARG-31st MEU is currently operating. tweet

OSINTdefender A U.S. Marine Corps KC-130J Super Hercules assigned to Marine Aerial Refueler Transport Squadron 152 (VMGR-152), under Marine Aircraft Group 12 (MAG-12), 1st Marine Aircraft Wing (1st MAW), lands on an unimproved runway for the first time at Japan Air Self-Defense Force Gifu Airfield in Southern Japan, assisted by air traffic control mobile team members with Marine Air Control Squadron 4 (MACS-4), April 9, 2026. tweet

↩️ Reply to ResistanceTrench mirror: "🇺🇸🇮🇩⚡️U.S. and Indonesia are discussing permission for U.S. military to access Indonesian airspace. Hegseth to meet with Indonesian Defense Minister Sjafrie Sjamsoeddin today" 🇮🇩 🇺🇸 ⚡️ Indonesia claims to be “non-aligned,” but under Prabowo it keeps moving closer to Washington Indonesia Defense Minister signed the Major Defense Cooperation Partnership (MDCP) with the Pentagon. The deal focuses on military modernization, joint training, maritime security, autonomous systems, and interoperability -- especially around the Strait of Malacca, one of the world’s most strategic sea lanes. In practice, this strengthens Washington’s footprint in a region Indonesia claims to guard independently. Adding to the controversy, reports emerged of a draft Letter of Intent that would ease U.S. military overflight access through Indonesian airspace. Officials insist it is only preliminary and sovereignty remains protected, but many see the trend clearly. Prabowo’s regime keeps stumbling -- from joining Trump’s Board of Peace, pledging 8,000 troops for Gaza, approving controversial cross-border data access for U.S. interests, and now handing Washington deeper military ties over the Malacca lifeline.

⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 Politico: The maximum that Iran is willing to concede in negotiations doesn’t meet the minimum that the U.S. is willing to accept. "I think that's exactly right. I think the White House is trying to take away the leverage that Iran believes it has, the leverage that it has projected onto the world. You know, the the challenge, and I think the vice president laid it out a little bit in the interview there, is this threshold for pain that Iran does have. This is existential for them. And he took on an enormously difficult task with these negotiations because what I'm hearing from sources close to the White House is that the maximum that Iran is willing to give does not meet the minimum that The United States is willing to accept." @wfwitness
